page: Amazon Web Services i7ie.6xlarge is a storage-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 24 vCPUs (12 physical cores with hyperthreading) operating at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz, supported by a 320 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It includes 15,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps, running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results indicate strong capabilities in memory and data processing. The instance achieves top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression at 34.65 GB/sec and Geekbench single-core execution with a score of 2314.0. Memory write bandwidth for large blocks is strong at 107.16 GB/sec, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression reaches 168.6 GB/sec. Additionally, the CPU's Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) support contributes to strong text generation speeds in small LLM inference workloads, processing 415.29 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i7ie.6xlarge offers high resource density by bundling a massive 15 TB local NVMe SSD with 192 GB of DDR5 memory. This high storage-to-compute ratio reduces the need for external block storage attachments, optimizing costs for data-heavy operations. However, a potential tradeoff is the lack of a dedicated GPU, which limits its utility for heavy parallel computing or large-scale deep learning training, despite its strong CPU-based inference capabilities.

This instance is highly suited for storage-intensive and memory-heavy workloads. It is optimized for large-scale relational and non-relational databases, data warehousing, high-throughput static web serving, and real-time analytics. It is also well-suited for data compression pipelines, software compilation, and CPU-bound machine learning inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.6xlarge is a storage-optimized instance powered by a 24-vCPU Intel Xeon 8559C processor running at 3.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 192.0 GB of DDR5 memory and a substantial 15,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance delivers strong performance across data-intensive benchmarks, including top-tier single-threaded gzip compression at 34.65 GB/sec and a Geekbench single-core score of 2314.0. Its memory write bandwidth is strong at 107.16 GB/sec, and it achieves 415.29 tokens/sec for small LLM text generation. Lacking a dedicated GPU, this instance relies on its CPU's Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for acceleration. The i7ie.6xlarge is highly efficient for workloads requiring massive local storage and high memory capacity, such as large databases, data warehousing, high-throughput web serving, and real-time data analytics.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.6xlarge is a storage-optimized instance featuring 24 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 15,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for database and data warehousing workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.6xlarge is a storage-optimized instance with 24 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB DDR5 RAM, and 15,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A storage-optimized platform combining massive local NVMe SSD capacity with high-speed DDR5 memory for data-intensive database and analytical workloads.
bullet_points: 24 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor at 3.6 GHz
192 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory (8 GB per core)
15,000 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core performance and data compression speeds
Optimized for large databases, data warehousing, and real-time analytics
